wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for OpenShadow focused on installation, setup, and basic usage. Explain the project's purpose as a desktop AI agent that works autonomously in your workspace. Document system requirements, installation steps using npm, and first-run configuration including provider setup with Chinese LLM providers (MiniMax, DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM). Include practical examples of creating your first agent, writing jian notes, and running basic tasks. Provide quick start commands for development mode and production builds. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting tips. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the memory architecture overview focused on the multi-tiered knowledge retention system. Explain the overall design principles including session summaries, daily/weekly compilation, and long-term storage hierarchy. Document the MemoryManager as the central orchestrator coordinating between SessionSummaryManager, MemoryCompiler, and DeepMemoryManager. Detail the directory structure organization (summaries, daily, weekly, longterm, facts) and data flow patterns. Include configuration options like turnsPerSummary, model selection, and memory directory paths. Provide examples of how sessions trigger different compilation processes and how compiled memories are injected into conversation context.

Document yuan templates (hanako, ming, butter) and their personality differences. Include practical examples of configuring different agent types with specific models, memory policies, and workspace paths. Detail template variables like {{userName}} and {{agentName}} in identity.md files. Address configuration validation, migration patterns, and troubleshooting common configuration errors.

Address the multi-process architecture with main process, renderer processes, and server processes. Explain the plugin-based extensibility model and how different subsystems interact. Provide an architectural decision rationale covering technology stack choices and design trade-offs.

# OpenShadow: Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

OpenShadow is an advanced AI-powered desktop application that serves as an autonomous digital assistant and coding companion. Built with Electron, React, and TypeScript, it provides a sophisticated platform for AI agents to perform tasks, manage files, execute code, and interact with various external services.

### Purpose Statement
OpenShadow aims to be "Your AI shadow that works in the dark" - providing autonomous patrol, task execution, and file monitoring capabilities through intelligent AI agents that can operate independently while maintaining security and user control.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Autonomous Task Execution**: Enable AI agents to perform complex workflows without constant human intervention
- **Multi-Platform Integration**: Support connections to various messaging platforms (Feishu, QQ, WeChat) and AI providers
- **Secure File Operations**: Provide sandboxed file system access with granular permission controls
- **Intelligent Memory System**: Implement persistent memory with daily/weekly/long-term knowledge retention
- **Plugin Architecture**: Offer extensible plugin system for custom tools and integrations

### Target Audience
- Developers seeking AI-assisted coding environments
- Teams requiring automated workflow orchestration
- Users needing intelligent personal assistants with file system access
- Organizations looking for customizable AI agent platforms

## 2. Technical Architecture

OpenShadow follows a modular, event-driven architecture built around several core components:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Electron Main Process] --> B[HanaEngine Core]
    B --> C[Agent Manager]
    B --> D[Session Coordinator]
    B --> E[Config Coordinator]
    B --> F[Channel Manager]
    B --> G[Plugin Manager]
    
    C --> H[Individual Agents]
    H --> I[Tool Registry]
    H --> J[Memory System]
    H --> K[LLM Client]
    
    L[React Desktop UI] --> M[WebSocket Client]
    M --> N[HTTP Server]
    N --> O[API Routes]
    O --> B
    
    P[Plugin System] --> Q[Builtin Plugins]
    P --> R[Community Plugins]
    P --> S[Custom Plugins]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Engine Layer**: The `HanaEngine` class serves as the central orchestrator, managing all subsystems including agents, sessions, configuration, and plugins.

**Agent System**: Each agent operates independently with its own identity, personality, memory, and tool set. Agents can communicate through channels and coordinate complex workflows.

**Session Management**: Handles conversation lifecycle, message persistence, and context management across multiple concurrent sessions.

**Plugin Architecture**: Supports three tiers of plugins - builtin (core functionality), community (third-party), and custom (user-developed) - with different trust levels and capabilities.

### Design Patterns

- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Uses EventEmitter pattern for loose coupling between components
- **Factory Pattern**: Creates specialized instances (agents, tools, providers) based on configuration
- **Observer Pattern**: Implements reactive updates across UI and backend components
- **Strategy Pattern**: Allows pluggable implementations for different AI providers and storage backends

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant UI as React UI
    participant WS as WebSocket
    participant API as HTTP API
    participant Engine as HanaEngine
    participant Agent as Agent Instance
    participant Tools as Tool Registry
    
    UI->>+WS: Send Message
    WS->>+API: Forward Request
    API->>+Engine: Process Request
    Engine->>+Agent: Execute Prompt
    Agent->>+Tools: Call Tool
    Tools-->>-Agent: Return Result
    Agent-->>-Engine: Generate Response
    Engine-->>-API: Stream Response
    API-->>-WS: Forward Data
    WS-->>-UI: Update Interface
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Desktop Application Entry**: `desktop/main.ts` - Electron main process initialization with first-run wizard and window management

**Server Entry Point**: `server/main.ts` - HTTP server setup with API routes, WebSocket handling, and background job scheduling

**Core Engine**: `core/engine.ts` - Central orchestrator managing all subsystems and providing unified API surface

### Core Modules

**Configuration Management**: `core/config.ts` - Multi-layered configuration system supporting environment variables, config files, and runtime overrides

**Agent System**: `core/agent.ts` - Individual agent implementation with memory, tools, and personality management

**Plugin Manager**: `core/plugin-manager.ts` - Dynamic plugin loading with security boundaries and capability-based access control

**Tool Registry**: `core/tool-registry.ts` - Centralized tool discovery, registration, and execution framework

### Configuration Approach

The application uses a hierarchical configuration system:
- **Environment Variables**: For sensitive data like API keys
- **Config Files**: YAML/JSON for persistent settings
- **Runtime Overrides**: Dynamic configuration changes during operation
- **Per-Agent Settings**: Individual agent customization

### External Dependencies

Key dependencies include:
- **@mariozechner/pi-ai**: AI agent framework integration
- **hono**: Lightweight HTTP framework for API endpoints
- **better-sqlite3**: Embedded database for session and memory storage
- **react + zustand**: Frontend state management
- **electron**: Cross-platform desktop application framework

### Integration Points

**AI Provider Abstraction**: Pluggable support for OpenAI, Anthropic, local models, and custom providers through standardized interfaces

**Channel System**: Unified interface for messaging platforms (Feishu, QQ, WeChat) enabling cross-platform communication

**File System Security**: Sandbox-based file access with configurable permissions and audit logging

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class HanaEngine {
        +agentManager: AgentManager
        +sessionCoordinator: SessionCoordinator
        +configCoordinator: ConfigCoordinator
        +pluginManager: PluginManager
        +createSession()
        +promptSession()
        +switchAgent()
    }
    
    class Agent {
        +id: string
        +name: string
        +memory: MemorySystem
        +tools: ToolRegistry
        +executePrompt()
        +buildSystemPrompt()
    }
    
    class PluginManager {
        +plugins: Map~string, Plugin~
        +loadAll()
        +activatePlugin()
        +getLoadedTools()
    }
    
    HanaEngine --> Agent
    HanaEngine --> PluginManager
    Agent --> ToolRegistry
    PluginManager --> ToolRegistry
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Intelligent Memory System**: Persistent knowledge retention with automatic summarization, fact extraction, and contextual recall across sessions

**Multi-Modal AI Interaction**: Support for text, images, audio, and video processing through unified provider interfaces

**Automated Workflows**: Scheduled tasks, cron jobs, and trigger-based automation for repetitive operations

**Cross-Platform Messaging**: Real-time communication through integrated messaging channels with message routing and formatting

### Implementation Highlights

**Security Sandbox**: `core/sandbox/` - Comprehensive file system and command execution sandboxing with configurable policies

**Plugin Framework**: `core/plugin-manager.ts` - Extensible architecture supporting tools, routes, skills, and UI extensions

**Real-time Communication**: WebSocket-based streaming for live responses and progress updates

**State Management**: `desktop/src/react/stores/` - Reactive state management with Zustand for efficient UI updates

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialized
    Initialized --> LoadingPlugins: Start
    LoadingPlugins --> Ready: All Loaded
    Ready --> ProcessingRequest: User Input
    ProcessingRequest --> ExecutingTools: Tool Calls
    ExecutingTools --> GeneratingResponse: Tools Complete
    GeneratingResponse --> StreamingResponse: LLM Streaming
    StreamingResponse --> Ready: Response Complete
    
    Ready --> MemoryCompaction: Scheduled
    MemoryCompaction --> Ready: Compaction Done
    
    Ready --> PluginActivation: Event Trigger
    PluginActivation --> Ready: Activation Complete
```

**Memory Pipeline**: Automated compilation of conversation summaries into daily, weekly, and long-term knowledge bases with semantic search capabilities

**Tool Ecosystem**: Rich set of built-in tools for file operations, web browsing, terminal access, and browser automation, plus extensible plugin system for custom functionality

**Multi-Agent Coordination**: Support for sub-agents and workflow orchestration enabling complex multi-step operations with proper error handling and resource management

---
